source.title: Endocrine Treatment of Gender-Dysphoric/Gender-Incongruent Persons: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline

signals: maintain sex hormone levels within the normal range

extraction.summary: Evidence-based guidelines for endocrine treatment of gender-dysphoric/gender-incongruent persons. Addresses hormonal therapy for adolescents and adults seeking gender affirmation, with emphasis on multidisciplinary care and safety monitoring.

population.age_criteria: Adolescents with Tanner Stage G2/B2 puberty for suppression therapy; gender-affirming hormones typically by age 16; surgery delayed until age 18
population.risk_criteria: Confirmed persistent gender dysphoria/gender incongruence by qualified mental health professional
population.disease_definition: Gender dysphoria/gender incongruence - dissatisfaction with designated gender requiring multidisciplinary evaluation
population.exclusions: Prepubertal children excluded from hormone therapy; conditions other than gender dysphoria that may not benefit from treatment
population.severity_staging: Tanner staging used for pubertal development assessment

key_thresholds.context: Most adolescents have mental capacity for informed consent by this age
Legal age of majority required
During first year of therapy, then 1-2 times yearly
For pubertal development monitoring during treatment
DSM-5 criteria for diagnosis in adolescents and adults
ICD-10 criteria for transsexualism diagnosis
Initiation of physical puberty in boys
Most adolescents have sufficient mental capacity by this age
Standard recommendation for sufficient mental capacity
Normal male range for transgender males
Premenopausal female range for transgender females
For transgender females on estrogen therapy
Upper limit to avoid erythrocytosis risk
Target range for injections; measure midway between injections
Suppression target
Premenopausal female range; should not exceed peak physiologic range
Minimum age for surgery affecting fertility
Required before genital surgery (unless contraindicated)
Continuous full-time living in new gender role before surgery
Complete duration before considering breast augmentation
Puberty suppression eligibility
Monitoring for prolactinoma risk in hormone therapy
Assessment before and during hormone therapy

recommendations.recommendation: Only trained mental health professionals meeting specific criteria should diagnose gender dysphoria
recommendations.notes: Requires competence in DSM/ICD, psychiatric conditions, and transgender-specific assessment

recommendations.recommendation: Recommend against puberty blocking and gender-affirming hormone treatment in prepubertal children
Begin pubertal hormone suppression after physical changes of puberty appear
Use GnRH analogues to suppress pubertal hormones where indicated
Maintain physiologic levels of gender-appropriate hormones in adults
recommendations.notes: Monitor for adverse effects

recommendations.recommendation: Only trained MHPs meeting specific criteria should diagnose GD/gender incongruence in adults
recommendations.notes: Requires competence in DSM/ICD, ability to distinguish from similar conditions, training in psychiatric diagnosis, and regular professional meeting attendance

recommendations.recommendation: Only MHPs with specialized training in child/adolescent development should diagnose GD/gender incongruence in children and adolescents
recommendations.notes: Requires additional training in developmental psychology and knowledge of puberty blocking criteria

recommendations.recommendation: Clinicians inform and counsel all individuals seeking gender-affirming medical treatment regarding options for fertility preservation prior to initiating puberty suppression in adolescents and prior to treating with hormonal therapy
recommendations.notes: Applies to both adolescents and adults

recommendations.recommendation: Against puberty blocking and gender-affirming hormone treatment in prepubertal children with GD/gender incongruence
recommendations.notes: 85% of prepubertal children with GD do not remain gender dysphoric in adolescence

recommendations.recommendation: Adolescents meeting diagnostic criteria should initially undergo treatment to suppress pubertal development
recommendations.notes: Must meet Table 5 criteria and be requesting treatment

recommendations.recommendation: Begin pubertal hormone suppression after first physical changes of puberty (Tanner stages G2/B2)
recommendations.notes: Emotional reaction to first pubertal changes has diagnostic value

recommendations.recommendation: GnRH analogues are used to suppress pubertal hormones where indicated
recommendations.notes: Most effective method for pubertal suppression

recommendations.recommendation: Confirm diagnostic criteria of GD/gender incongruence before beginning treatment
recommendations.notes: Essential prerequisite for hormone therapy

recommendations.recommendation: Evaluate medical conditions that can be exacerbated by hormones before treatment
recommendations.notes: Address contraindications and risk factors

recommendations.recommendation: Initiate sex hormones using gradually increasing dose schedule after multidisciplinary team confirmation
recommendations.notes: Most adolescents have capacity by age 16 years

recommendations.recommendation: Monitor clinical development every 3-6 months, laboratory parameters every 6-12 months
recommendations.notes: During sex hormone treatment

recommendations.recommendation: Measure hormone levels to ensure suppression and maintain physiologic range
recommendations.notes: For treatment monitoring and safety

recommendations.recommendation: Regular clinical evaluation and laboratory monitoring of sex steroid hormone levels every 3 months during first year, then 1-2 times yearly
recommendations.notes: Monitor for physical changes and adverse effects

recommendations.recommendation: Periodically monitor prolactin levels in transgender females treated with estrogens
recommendations.notes: At baseline and annually during transition, then every 2 years

recommendations.recommendation: Evaluate for cardiovascular risk factors using fasting lipid profiles and diabetes screening
recommendations.notes: Regular monitoring according to established guidelines

recommendations.recommendation: Obtain BMD measurements when risk factors for osteoporosis exist, especially after gonadectomy
recommendations.notes: Particularly important if sex hormone therapy is stopped

recommendations.recommendation: Pursue genital gender-affirming surgery only after both MHP and endocrine clinician agree surgery is medically necessary
recommendations.notes: Requires dual approval from mental health professional and hormone prescriber

recommendations.recommendation: Approve genital gender-affirming surgery only after completion of at least 1 year of hormone treatment
recommendations.notes: Unless hormone therapy not desired or medically contraindicated

recommendations.recommendation: Refer for genital surgery when individual has satisfactory social role change, satisfaction with hormonal effects, and desires surgical changes
recommendations.notes: Requires meeting all three criteria

recommendations.recommendation: Delay gender-affirming genital surgery involving gonadectomy/hysterectomy until patient is at least 18 years old
recommendations.notes: Or legal age of majority in country

recommendations.recommendation: Determine timing of breast surgery for transgender males based on physical and mental health status
recommendations.notes: Insufficient evidence for specific age requirement

recommendations.recommendation: Use GnRH agonists for puberty suppression in adolescents with gender dysphoria
recommendations.notes: Based on clinical experience and follow-up studies

recommendations.recommendation: Monitor bone density during GnRH agonist treatment
recommendations.notes: Due to potential bone loss effects

recommendations.recommendation: Consider testosterone therapy for female-to-male transgender individuals
recommendations.notes: Multiple formulations available

recommendations.recommendation: Use estrogen therapy for male-to-female transgender individuals
recommendations.notes: With or without anti-androgens

recommendations.recommendation: Monitor prolactin levels in transgender women receiving estrogen therapy
recommendations.notes: Risk of prolactinoma with long-term estrogen use

recommendations.recommendation: Assess bone mineral density in transgender patients
recommendations.notes: Important for both male-to-female and female-to-male patients

recommendations.recommendation: Monitor cardiovascular risk factors during hormone therapy
recommendations.notes: Includes lipids, thrombotic risk assessment

medications.indication: Gender-affirming hormone therapy for transgender males
Gender-affirming hormone therapy for transgender females
Pubertal suppression in gender dysphoric adolescents
Gender-affirming hormone therapy for transgender females
Gender-affirming hormone therapy for transgender males

red_flags: Medical conditions that can be exacerbated by hormone depletion
Conditions with similar features to gender dysphoria (e.g., body dysmorphic disorder)
Inadequate mental health evaluation or support
Severe psychiatric comorbidity interfering with treatment
Unrealistically high expectations for treatment outcomes
Uncontrolled mental health conditions that could interfere with treatment
Medical contraindications to GnRH analog treatment
Conditions mimicking gender dysphoria (body dysmorphic disorder, body identity integrity disorder)
Thromboembolic events in transgender females on estrogen
Erythrocytosis (hematocrit >50%) in transgender males on testosterone
Signs of incomplete gonadal axis suppression (continued menses, erections, progressive hair growth)
Persistent prolactin elevation despite stable/reduced estrogen levels
Significant uncontrolled medical or mental health concerns
Ambivalence about surgery or hormone effects
Dissatisfaction with social and physical outcomes during hormone transition
Significant mood changes or psychiatric deterioration
extraction.follow_up: Clinical evaluation every 3-6 months during treatment; hormone levels every 3 months in first year, then 1-2 times yearly; cardiovascular and metabolic monitoring; bone density assessment when risk factors present
extraction.escalation_path: Multidisciplinary team required for adolescent treatment; collaboration between endocrinologist, mental health professional, and surgeon for surgical decisions; expert team management for complex cases
